name: Garlic Rosemary Shrimp New Orleans
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh rosemary, stems removed
1/2 teaspoon red chili peppers, crushed and dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 12-inch cast iron skillet over medium heat.
2. Add garlic cloves and sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant but not browned.
3. Add shrimp and bay leaves; continue to sauté, shaking the pan frequently, until the shrimp are pink and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
4. Stir in oregano, rosemary, crushed red pepper, salt, and pepper.
5. Pour in white wine, vinegar, and lemon juice; simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes until the shrimp are tender and garlic is softened.
6. Adjust seasonings with salt and pepper if needed, then sprinkle generously with nutmeg before serving.
7. Serve over pasta tossed with butter and Parmesan cheese, with cracked black pepper on the side. The dish also tastes great at room temperature the next day.
